response.justification: Wei Chen fails the most critical minimum qualification for this role: 8 years of C++ programming experience. C++ does not appear in their listed skills section at all, and their actual C++ exposure amounts to roughly 6 months at National Instruments and a single bachelor thesis project — a gap of approximately 7.5 years against the hard requirement. Their career is overwhelmingly web-oriented (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), and while they have genuine distributed systems exposure from Sociomantic's high-throughput microservices environment, there is no cloud compute platform depth, no cloud architecture ownership, and no evidence of technical leadership at the cross-team infrastructure level this role demands.
strengths: Proven distributed systems and high-throughput work at Sociomantic — millions of HTTP requests/second, async I/O, low-latency design
Strong engineering-culture fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and active mentoring through workshops and code katas
Broad DevOps and infrastructure exposure across Kubernetes, Docker, GitLab CI, AWS CDK, and multiple cloud-adjacent toolchains
concerns: C++ experience is critically absent — the minimum bar is 8 years; the resume shows roughly 6 months total and omits it from the skills list entirely
No cloud compute platform or cloud architecture ownership — AWS CDK at Optilyz is infrastructure-as-code tooling, not platform-level cloud design
Technical leadership is informal and project-scoped with no evidence of driving cross-team infrastructure strategy or mentoring cloud/systems engineers
